message:
feat(fwc): add agent session registration and context persistence

CUAL-M.1: session.rs with file-backed session store for agent identity
tracking. Sessions persist goals, zones, operations counters, and
arbitrary context across context rotations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.3","title":"[CUAL-D.3] Idempotent undo for reversible operations","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc undo' that reverses a previous operation where the operation type supports it (e.g., create -> delete, enable -> disable).

## Background
Some operations have natural inverses:
- github.create_issue -> github.close_issue (or delete)
- slack.send_message -> slack.delete_message
- todoist.create_task -> todoist.delete_task
- kubernetes.create_pod -> kubernetes.delete_pod
- connector.enable -> connector.disable

## How It Works
1. Look up operation in history
2. Check if operation has a declared inverse in manifest (new manifest field: inverse_operation)
3. If inverse exists: construct inverse input from original output (e.g., use created issue ID)
4. If no declared inverse: suggest manual reversal steps
5. Show preview and require confirmation
6. Execute inverse operation
7. Log undo in history with reference to original

## Manifest Extension
[provides.operations.'github.create_issue']
inverse_operation = 'github.delete_issue'
inverse_input_mapping = { 'owner' = '$.input.owner', 'repo' = '$.input.repo', 'issue_number' = '$.output.issue.number' }

## Deliverables
- Inverse operation lookup from manifest
- Input mapping from original output to inverse input
- fwc undo command
- Manual reversal guidance for non-reversible operations
- Tests: create-undo cycle, non-reversible handling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","history","reversible","undo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.055862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.4","title":"[CUAL-D.4] Operation result diffing and comparison","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc diff' that compares two operation results to show what changed, useful for monitoring state changes and debugging.

## Commands
- fwc diff <entry_id_1> <entry_id_2>     # Compare two invocations
- fwc diff --last-two github.list_issues  # Compare last two runs of same operation
- fwc diff --baseline <entry_id>          # Compare latest vs saved baseline

## Output
Shows structured diff of JSON outputs:
  github.list_issues (2026-03-09 14:00 vs 2026-03-09 15:00):
  + issues[3]: {number: 44, title: 'New bug', state: 'open'}
  ~ issues[0].labels: ['bug'] -> ['bug', 'priority']
  - issues[1]: {number: 41, title: 'Fixed issue'} (removed/closed)

## Why Agents Need This
- Monitor state changes: 'what changed in my GitHub issues since last check?'
- Verify side effects: 'did my create_issue actually add the expected issue?'
- Debug regressions: 'why is this connector returning different data now?'

## Deliverables
- JSON diff engine (structural, not textual)
- fwc diff command
- Baseline save/load for periodic comparison
- TOON and JSON output modes
- Tests: identical, added, removed, modified, nested diff","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.665709Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:46:16.265344Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:46:16.265328Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["comparison","cual","diff","history"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:18.665709Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1215,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.4.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-D.4 JSON diff engine complete: json_diff.rs (350 lines, 25 tests). Structural diff between two JSON values with path-aware change tracking. Supports: object field add/remove/modify, array item changes, deeply nested paths, scalar type changes. Outputs: summary string, rendered diff lines, serializable DiffResult. 1049 fwc tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:46:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","title":"[CUAL-E] Output Transformation & Extraction","description":"Track E: Output Transformation and Extraction. Phase 1 (1g7z0.2) provides TOON/JSON output modes. This track adds surgical extraction: jq-style field selection (--extract '.issues[0].title'), Handlebars template rendering (--template), table/CSV/TSV/Markdown formats, and streaming-friendly NDJSON mode. Agents need to extract specific fields without parsing entire JSON blobs in-context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:23:49.048843Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:23:49.048843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","format","jq","output","template"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:23:49.048843Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","title":"[CUAL-E.1] jq-style field extraction with --extract flag","description":"## Goal
Add --extract (or --jq) flag to all fwc commands that produces JSON output, enabling surgical field extraction without agents needing to parse full JSON blobs.

## Commands
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues[0].title'
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues | length'
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues[].{title,number,state}'
- fwc search 'send message' --extract '.[0].operation_id'

## Implementation
Use jaq crate (Rust implementation of jq) for filter evaluation. The filter is applied to the JSON output of any command AFTER successful execution.

## Why Agents Need This
Agents pay per token. A full GitHub issue listing might be 5000 tokens. The agent only needs the issue number (3 tokens). --extract reduces output to exactly what the agent needs, saving 99%+ of token budget on data-heavy operations.

## Edge Cases
- Invalid filter expression: return error with jq syntax help
- Filter returns empty: return null with exit code 0 (not an error)
- Filter returns multiple values: return JSON array
- Non-JSON command output: error with 'command does not produce JSON output'

## Deliverables
- jaq integration as output post-processor
- --extract flag on all JSON-producing commands
- Error handling for invalid filters
- Tests: field selection, array indexing, object construction, length, empty results","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberAspen","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.534579Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:11:48.794515Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","extraction","jq","output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.534579Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1193,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: Use the 'jaq-core' and 'jaq-std' crates (Rust jq implementation). These are already well-maintained and support the full jq filter language. The --extract flag should be a post-processing step applied after the command produces its JSON output — this means it works with ANY command that produces JSON, not just invoke. Performance consideration: for large outputs (100KB+), stream through jaq rather than loading entire JSON into memory. Edge case: if the command produces TOON output (not JSON), --extract should emit an error suggesting --json flag.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:34:20Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","title":"[CUAL-E.1] jq-style field extraction with --extract flag","description":"## Goal
Add --extract (or --jq) flag to all fwc commands that produces JSON output, enabling surgical field extraction without agents needing to parse full JSON blobs.

## Commands
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues[0].title'
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues | length'
- fwc invoke github list_issues --extract '.issues[].{title,number,state}'
- fwc search 'send message' --extract '.[0].operation_id'

## Implementation
Use jaq crate (Rust implementation of jq) for filter evaluation. The filter is applied to the JSON output of any command AFTER successful execution.

## Why Agents Need This
Agents pay per token. A full GitHub issue listing might be 5000 tokens. The agent only needs the issue number (3 tokens). --extract reduces output to exactly what the agent needs, saving 99%+ of token budget on data-heavy operations.

## Edge Cases
- Invalid filter expression: return error with jq syntax help
- Filter returns empty: return null with exit code 0 (not an error)
- Filter returns multiple values: return JSON array
- Non-JSON command output: error with 'command does not produce JSON output'

## Deliverables
- jaq integration as output post-processor
- --extract flag on all JSON-producing commands
- Error handling for invalid filters
- Tests: field selection, array indexing, object construction, length, empty results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberAspen","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.534579Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:46:42.275554Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:46:42.275534Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in tracked HEAD (fwc --extract/jaq); verified with cargo check -p fwc and cargo test -p fwc","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","extraction","jq","output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.534579Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1193,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: Use the 'jaq-core' and 'jaq-std' crates (Rust jq implementation). These are already well-maintained and support the full jq filter language. The --extract flag should be a post-processing step applied after the command produces its JSON output — this means it works with ANY command that produces JSON, not just invoke. Performance consideration: for large outputs (100KB+), stream through jaq rather than loading entire JSON into memory. Edge case: if the command produces TOON output (not JSON), --extract should emit an error suggesting --json flag.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.2","title":"[CUAL-E.2] Handlebars template rendering with --template flag","description":"## Goal
Add --template flag for rendering operation results through Handlebars templates, producing human/agent-optimized text output.

## Commands
- fwc invoke github list_issues --template '{{#each issues}}#{{number}}: {{title}} ({{state}})\n{{/each}}'
- fwc invoke github get_issue --template 'Issue #{{issue.number}}: {{issue.title}} by {{issue.user.login}}'
- fwc invoke github list_issues --template-file issue_summary.hbs

## Built-in Helpers
- {{json .}} - pretty-print as JSON
- {{compact .}} - single-line JSON
- {{count items}} - array length
- {{truncate text 80}} - truncate string
- {{timeago timestamp}} - relative time
- {{if_eq a b}} - equality check

## Why Agents Need This
Templates let agents format output for specific downstream consumers: Slack messages, commit messages, issue descriptions, email bodies. Instead of extracting fields and string-building in the agent context (expensive), the template runs on the output directly.

## Deliverables
- Handlebars integration (handlebars-rust crate)
- --template inline and --template-file from file
- Built-in helper functions
- Tests: simple interpolation, iteration, conditionals, helpers, file templates","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.797376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:57:26.582953Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T12:57:26.582940Z","close_reason":"Implemented Handlebars output post-processing for fwc with --template/--template-file, built-in helpers, strict validation, template metadata, and test coverage. Verified via rch exec cargo fmt -p fwc --check, rch exec cargo check -p fwc --all-targets, rch exec cargo test -p fwc, rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets, and rch exec cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings. Repo-wide cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","handlebars","output","template"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:53.797376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.3","title":"[CUAL-E.3] Table, CSV, TSV, and Markdown output format modes","description":"## Goal
Add --format table/csv/tsv/markdown flags for tabular output of list/search results.

## Commands
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format table                   # ASCII table
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format csv                     # CSV
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format tsv                     # TSV (pipe-friendly)
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format markdown                # Markdown table
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format table --columns number,title,state  # Select columns

## Auto-Detection
For operations returning arrays of objects, auto-detect column names from object keys. For nested objects, flatten with dot notation (user.login, labels[0].name).

## Column Selection
- --columns: explicit column list
- --no-columns: suppress column headers
- --sort-by: sort by column
- --limit: limit rows

## Why Agents Need This
- Table: quick visual summary for human review
- CSV/TSV: pipe to other tools (awk, cut, sort)
- Markdown: paste into issues, PRs, docs
- Column selection: minimize output to exactly needed fields

## Deliverables
- Table formatter using comfy-table or similar
- CSV/TSV serializer
- Markdown table formatter
- Auto-column detection from JSON array output
- --columns, --sort-by, --limit flags
- Tests: various data shapes, nested objects, empty arrays","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:54.054428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:23:03.826560Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:23:03.826540Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["csv","cual","format","output","table"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:54.054428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1211,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-E.3 tabular output complete: format_table.rs (300 lines, 22 tests). Added Table/CSV/TSV/Markdown to OutputFormat enum. Global --columns, --sort-by, --limit, --no-headers flags. Features: auto-column detection from JSON arrays, dot notation for nested fields, array index notation, CSV quoting, Markdown pipe escaping, sort+limit, wrapper object detection. 923 fwc tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:23:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.4","title":"[CUAL-E.4] Streaming NDJSON output for pipe-friendly line-delimited JSON","description":"## Goal
Add --format ndjson for newline-delimited JSON output, optimized for streaming and piping between commands.

## Behavior
- Each result item emitted as a single JSON line (no array wrapping)
- Flushed immediately (no buffering) for real-time streaming
- Compatible with jq --slurp for re-assembly
- Works with streaming/event operations (each event = one line)

## Commands
- fwc invoke github list_issues --format ndjson | jq '.title'
- fwc events tail slack --format ndjson | fwc pipe github.create_issue
- fwc batch ops.jsonl --format ndjson  # Each result on its own line

## Why Agents Need This
NDJSON is the standard format for streaming JSON between processes. It enables:
- Real-time processing of streaming events
- Piping between fwc commands
- Integration with standard Unix tools (jq, grep, wc)
- Partial result consumption (don't wait for full array)

## Deliverables
- NDJSON output formatter
- Immediate flush per line
- Integration with streaming/event commands
- Tests: list output, streaming output, pipe scenarios","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:54.375938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:38:44.721419Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:38:44.721398Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","ndjson","output","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:27:54.375938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1213,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.5.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-E.4 NDJSON streaming complete: Added Ndjson format to OutputFormat enum. render_ndjson() emits each array item on its own line.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","title":"[CUAL-F] Pipeline & Composition Engine","description":"Track F: Pipeline and Composition Engine. Agents often need multi-step workflows: fetch GitHub issues, filter by label, post summary to Slack. Today this requires multiple fwc invoke calls with manual JSON plumbing between them. This track adds: two-operation pipe (A output feeds B input), named multi-step pipeline definitions in TOML, conditional branching, error handling/rollback, pipeline dry-run with cost/risk estimation, and a pre-built recipe library for common cross-connector workflows.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:24:08.389615Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:24:08.389615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["composition","cual","pipeline","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:24:08.389615Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.1","title":"[CUAL-F.1] Two-operation pipe: output of A feeds input of B","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc pipe' that chains two operations: the output of operation A becomes the input of operation B, with a JSON path mapping between them.

## Commands
- fwc pipe github.list_issues slack.send_message --map 'issues[0].title -> text, "#general" -> channel'
- fwc pipe github.search_issues todoist.create_task --map 'items[0].title -> content'
- fwc pipe jira.get_issue github.create_issue --map-file jira-to-github.json
- fwc pipe A B --dry-run  # Show what would be sent to B without executing

## Map Syntax
Simple: 'source.path -> target.field'
Multiple: 'a.x -> b.x, a.y -> b.y'
Literal: '"value" -> field'
Template: '"Issue: {{a.title}}" -> body'

## How It Works
1. Execute operation A with provided input
2. Apply mapping rules to transform A's output into B's input
3. Validate B's input against B's input_schema
4. Show preview if B is risky/dangerous, require approval
5. Execute operation B
6. Return B's output (with A's output available via --include-intermediate)

## Error Handling
- If A fails: report error, don't execute B
- If mapping produces invalid input for B: report validation error with fix suggestions
- If B fails: report error with A's output still available

## Deliverables
- Pipe executor with A -> mapping -> B flow
- Map expression parser and evaluator
- Map file format (JSON)
- Dry-run mode
- Approval gating for risky B operations
- Tests: happy path, mapping errors, A failure, B failure, approval","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.605886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:38:56.572964Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:38:56.572950Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["composition","cual","pipe","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.605886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1214,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Two-operation pipe engine (pipe.rs): MapRule, MappingSpec, parse_map_expression, parse_map_file, apply_mapping, resolve_json_path, set_target (nested), PipePlan. 50 tests. CLI wired as 'fwc pipe' with --map/--map-file/--dry-run flags. Commit 573e2d18.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:38:52Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.2","title":"[CUAL-F.2] Named multi-step pipeline definitions in TOML","description":"## Goal
Define reusable multi-step pipelines as TOML files that chain N operations with mappings, conditionals, and error handling.

## Pipeline Definition Format
[pipeline]
name = 'notify-on-new-issues'
description = 'Check for new GitHub issues and post to Slack'
version = '1.0'

[[steps]]
id = 'fetch'
operation = 'github.list_issues'
input = { owner = '{{params.owner}}', repo = '{{params.repo}}', state = 'open', since = '{{params.since}}' }

[[steps]]
id = 'notify'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { channel = '{{params.channel}}', text = 'New issues: {{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}}' }
depends_on = ['fetch']
condition = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}} > 0'

[params]
owner = { type = 'string', required = true }
repo = { type = 'string', required = true }
channel = { type = 'string', default = '#general' }
since = { type = 'string', default = '24h' }

## Commands
- fwc pipeline run notify-on-new-issues --param owner=octocat --param repo=hello-world
- fwc pipeline validate my-pipeline.toml
- fwc pipeline dry-run my-pipeline.toml --param ...
- fwc pipeline list                        # Show available pipelines
- fwc pipeline show my-pipeline            # Show pipeline details

## Pipeline Storage
- User pipelines: ~/.fwc/pipelines/*.toml
- Project pipelines: .fwc/pipelines/*.toml
- Built-in recipes: bundled in fwc binary

## Deliverables
- Pipeline TOML parser and validator
- Step executor with dependency resolution
- Template rendering for step inputs (Handlebars)
- Parameter binding with defaults and validation
- fwc pipeline command family
- Tests: multi-step, conditional, parameterized, validation errors","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.848832Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.848832Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","pipeline","toml","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.848832Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Define reusable multi-step pipelines as TOML files that chain N operations with mappings, conditionals, and error handling.

## Pipeline Definition Format
[pipeline]
name = 'notify-on-new-issues'

[[steps]]

[[steps]]

[params]
owner = { type = 'string', required = true }

channel = { type = 'string', default = '#general' }

## Commands
- fwc pipeline run notify-on-new-issues --param owner=octocat --param repo=hello-world
- fwc pipeline validate my-pipeline.toml
- fwc pipeline dry-run my-pipeline.toml --param ...
- fwc pipeline list                        # Show available pipelines
- fwc pipeline show my-pipeline            # Show pipeline details

## Pipeline Storage

- Project pipelines: .fwc/pipelines/*.toml

## Deliverables

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.3","title":"[CUAL-F.3] Pipeline conditional branching and error handling","description":"## Goal
Add conditional branching (if/then/else) and error handling (on_error) to pipeline definitions.

## Conditional Steps
[[steps]]
id = 'check-labels'
operation = 'github.get_issue'
input = { owner = '{{params.owner}}', repo = '{{params.repo}}', issue_number = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues[0].number}}' }
condition = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}} > 0'

[[steps]]
id = 'escalate'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { channel = '#urgent', text = 'Critical issue found\!' }
condition = '{{steps.check-labels.output.issue.labels | any("priority:critical")}}' 
depends_on = ['check-labels']

## Error Handling
[[steps]]
id = 'risky-op'
operation = 'github.create_issue'
input = { ... }
on_error = 'skip'  # skip | abort | retry(3) | fallback(step_id)

[[steps]]
id = 'fallback-notify'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { text = 'Failed to create issue: {{steps.risky-op.error}}' }
trigger = 'on_error(risky-op)'

## Deliverables
- Condition evaluator (simple expression language)
- Error handling modes: skip, abort, retry with count, fallback to another step
- Error-triggered steps
- Pipeline execution trace showing which steps ran, skipped, failed
- Tests: branching, error-skip, error-retry, error-fallback","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.087750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.087750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conditionals","cual","errors","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.087750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.4","title":"[CUAL-F.4] Pipeline dry-run with cost and risk estimation","description":"## Goal
Add pipeline dry-run that simulates execution without side effects and reports: estimated API calls, risk assessment, rate limit impact, and required capabilities.

## Commands
- fwc pipeline dry-run my-pipeline.toml --param owner=octocat
- fwc pipeline estimate my-pipeline.toml  # Cost/risk summary only

## Dry-Run Output
Pipeline: notify-on-new-issues (3 steps)
Step 1: github.list_issues     [safe]  ~1 API call, reads 'github.read' capability
Step 2: github.get_issue       [safe]  ~0-N API calls (conditional), reads 'github.read'
Step 3: slack.send_message     [risky] ~0-1 API call (conditional), requires 'slack.write'

Risk Assessment: MEDIUM (step 3 writes to external service)
Rate Limit Impact: github.read: 2/5000 remaining, slack.write: 1/100 remaining
Required Capabilities: github.read, slack.write
Required Approvals: step 3 requires policy approval

## Deliverables
- Pipeline simulator that resolves steps without executing
- API call estimator based on conditionals
- Risk aggregator across pipeline steps
- Rate limit impact calculator
- Required capability/approval summary
- Tests: various pipeline shapes, conditional estimation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.305512Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.305512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","dry-run","estimation","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.305512Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.5","title":"[CUAL-F.5] Pre-built pipeline recipe library for common cross-connector workflows","description":"## Goal
Ship fwc with a library of pre-built pipeline recipes for common agent workflows that work across connector combinations.

## Recipe Categories

### Development Workflows
- issue-to-branch: GitHub/Jira issue -> create branch -> post link back
- pr-review-notify: new PR -> summarize changes -> notify Slack/Discord
- deploy-announce: release tag -> deploy status -> notify team
- bug-triage: Sentry alert -> create issue -> assign -> notify

### Communication Workflows
- cross-post: Slack message -> Discord + Telegram
- digest-email: aggregate Slack messages -> send Gmail digest
- meeting-notes: notion/logseq doc -> send to attendees

### Data Workflows
- backup-to-s3: list items from service -> export -> upload to S3
- sync-contacts: HubSpot contacts -> Google Contacts
- analytics-report: Mixpanel data -> format -> send to Slack

### Operations Workflows
- health-check-all: check all connectors -> report to Slack
- rotate-secrets: list expiring -> rotate -> update configs -> notify

## Commands
- fwc recipe list                          # Show all built-in recipes
- fwc recipe show issue-to-branch          # Show recipe details
- fwc recipe run issue-to-branch --param ...  # Run with params
- fwc recipe export issue-to-branch > my-recipe.toml  # Export for customization

## Deliverables
- 15-20 built-in recipes covering the above categories
- Recipe registry with metadata (category, required connectors, risk level)
- fwc recipe command family
- Recipe customization via export + edit + re-import
- Tests: each recipe validates and dry-runs successfully","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.560474Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.560474Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","pipeline","recipes","templates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.560474Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
